Course.name: Introduction to Programming
Course.description: Foundational programming course covering basic concepts and Python syntax for beginners

Concept.name: Variables and Data Types
Concept.description: Understanding how to store and manipulate data using variables, integers, strings, and floats

Concept.name: Control Flow
Concept.description: Learning conditional statements (if/else) and logical operators for decision making

Concept.name: Loops
Concept.description: Iteration using for loops and while loops to repeat code execution

Exercise.name: Hello World
Exercise.description: Write your first Python program that prints a greeting message to the console

Exercise.name: Calculator
Exercise.description: Build a simple calculator that performs basic arithmetic operations on user input

Exercise.name: FizzBuzz
Exercise.description: Classic programming exercise using loops and conditionals to print numbers with special rules

Course.name: Data Structures
Course.description: Intermediate course covering essential data structures for efficient data organization and algorithms

Concept.name: Arrays and Lists
Concept.description: Understanding sequential data storage, indexing, and common list operations

Concept.name: Stacks and Queues
Concept.description: LIFO and FIFO data structures for managing ordered collections with specific access patterns

Concept.name: Hash Tables
Concept.description: Key-value storage with O(1) average lookup time using hashing functions

Concept.name: Trees
Concept.description: Hierarchical data structures including binary trees, BSTs, and tree traversal algorithms

Exercise.name: Implement a Stack
Exercise.description: Build a stack data structure with push, pop, and peek operations from scratch

Exercise.name: Two Sum Problem
Exercise.description: Find two numbers in an array that add up to a target using hash table optimization

Exercise.name: Binary Tree Traversal
Exercise.description: Implement inorder, preorder, and postorder traversal for binary trees recursively

Course.name: Machine Learning Fundamentals
Course.description: Introduction to machine learning concepts, algorithms, and practical applications using Python

Concept.name: Supervised Learning
Concept.description: Learning from labeled data to make predictions, including classification and regression tasks

Concept.name: Neural Networks
Concept.description: Artificial neural networks inspired by biological neurons, including perceptrons and activation functions

Concept.name: Model Evaluation
Concept.description: Techniques for assessing model performance including accuracy, precision, recall, and cross-validation

Concept.name: Feature Engineering
Concept.description: Creating and selecting meaningful features from raw data to improve model performance

Exercise.name: Linear Regression
Exercise.description: Implement linear regression from scratch and use scikit-learn to predict housing prices

Exercise.name: Image Classification
Exercise.description: Build a neural network to classify handwritten digits using the MNIST dataset

Exercise.name: Model Comparison
Exercise.description: Compare different classifiers (SVM, Random Forest, KNN) on a real-world dataset

Course.name: Web Development
Course.description: Full-stack web development course covering frontend, backend, and database technologies

Concept.name: HTML and CSS
Concept.description: Building web page structure with HTML and styling with CSS including flexbox and grid layouts

Concept.name: JavaScript Fundamentals
Concept.description: Client-side scripting, DOM manipulation, and event handling for interactive web pages

Concept.name: REST APIs
Concept.description: Designing and consuming RESTful web services with HTTP methods and JSON data format

Concept.name: Database Design
Concept.description: Relational database concepts, SQL queries, and schema design for web applications

Exercise.name: Portfolio Website
Exercise.description: Create a responsive personal portfolio website using HTML, CSS, and basic JavaScript

Exercise.name: Todo App
Exercise.description: Build a full-stack todo application with CRUD operations and database persistence

Exercise.name: API Integration
Exercise.description: Fetch and display data from a public API with error handling and loading states

Course.name: Algorithms
Course.description: Advanced course on algorithm design, analysis, and optimization techniques

Concept.name: Time Complexity
Concept.description: Big O notation and analyzing algorithm efficiency in terms of input size growth

Concept.name: Sorting Algorithms
Concept.description: Comparison-based sorting including quicksort, mergesort, and their time-space tradeoffs

Concept.name: Dynamic Programming
Concept.description: Solving complex problems by breaking them into overlapping subproblems with memoization

Concept.name: Graph Algorithms
Concept.description: BFS, DFS, shortest path algorithms, and minimum spanning trees for graph problems

Exercise.name: Implement QuickSort
Exercise.description: Write quicksort algorithm with different pivot selection strategies and analyze performance

Exercise.name: Fibonacci with DP
Exercise.description: Optimize Fibonacci calculation using dynamic programming and compare with naive recursion

Exercise.name: Shortest Path
Exercise.description: Implement Dijkstra's algorithm to find shortest paths in a weighted graph

Exercise.name: LeetCode Challenge
Exercise.description: Solve medium-difficulty LeetCode problems using learned algorithmic techniques
